build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: The player pilots a recognizable submarine freely through a 2D ocean cross-section with directional input, visible depth changes, pressure zones, depth meter, and hull gauge. Score 0 if the sub teleports, if depth does not affect the display or HUD, or if the submarine is only a labelled icon.

Sonar is an active system: player input sends expanding pulse rings that reveal hidden terrain, wrecks, caves, or creatures, while passive sonar shows moving contacts. Score 0 if the ocean is already fully visible, if sonar is just a static circle, or if pings reveal nothing new.

Oxygen depletes while submerged, changes warning state as it gets low, and can be refilled by surfacing or reaching air pockets. Running out should cause a blackout, forced ascent, or cargo loss. Score 0 if oxygen is static, if depletion is only text, or if there is no failure consequence.

The ocean includes at least three discoverable location types with distinct interaction: wrecks for salvage, caves for rare minerals, and coral or creature habitats for research. Score 0 if locations are identical markers, if they cannot be interacted with, or if rewards do not change state.

Depth pressure has real gameplay consequences: crossing the rated depth creates warnings, hull stress, and potential damage, while hull upgrades increase safe depth. Score 0 if the player can dive indefinitely, if hull never changes, or if pressure is only a background gradient.

At least three submarine upgrades are purchasable with salvage credits and visibly change gameplay, such as hull rating, oxygen capacity, sonar range, cargo hold, or headlight. Score 0 if upgrades are only listed or do not affect subsequent dives.

The ocean has distinct depth layers with unique visuals, content, and risk: shallow reef, mid-depth kelp or wreck zone, and deep abyss with bioluminescence or high pressure. Score 0 if the ocean is visually uniform or if depth layers do not change encounters.

The exploration loop is playable as one connected dive: leave surface, use sonar, discover a location, collect salvage, manage oxygen and pressure, surface, sell salvage, and upgrade. Score 0 if these steps are separate showcase states or if salvage and upgrades do not persist.

The full game loop connects title, dive launch, navigation, discovery, resource danger, surfacing, upgrading, and deeper repeat dives without restart. Score 0 if menus do not return to play or if state resets between sections.
The title screen uses a deep-ocean or bioluminescent composition with an interactive play button and visible transition to the dive. Score 0 if it is a generic title over simple shapes.
The visual style coherently supports ocean depth, submarine exploration, wrecks, creatures, sonar, and HUD. Score 0 if the game relies on labels and flat primitives to identify most objects.

The submarine, wrecks, caves, coral, creatures, depth layers, and HUD are recognizable without labels, with visual detail appropriate to underwater exploration. Score 0 if the sub is a plain oval/rectangle, if the ocean is a uniform fill, or if contacts are only text.

Sub propulsion, sonar pulses, reveal effects, oxygen warnings, pressure damage, salvage pickup, surfacing, and upgrade feedback animate over time. Score 0 if these events are instant toggles, blinking labels, or static before/after panels.
